talk_metadata.talk_title: Real-world analysis of non-small cell lung cancer variant level frequencies from liquid biopsy testing in diverse US populations
rhetorical_signals: Speaker said: "Technical success-wise, it is highly successful. I would say over 90% success rate."
Speaker said: "The discrepancies are likely related to cohort bias... It's not unexpected to see a difference in literature-reported prevalence as compared to observed rates."

unresolved_questions: What explains the lower KRAS and EGFR prevalence compared to trial cohorts?

talk_metadata.talk_title: Histological profile of lung cancer in never smokers and prognostic assessment of non-mucinous adenocarcinoma
rhetorical_signals: Speaker said: "We would love to have as much data as possible. But unfortunately, most data right now for lung cancer specifically does not systematically collect smoking status."

key_data_gaps: Limited external validation of deep learning model
Unclear biological basis for model risk stratification

crispro_opportunity.description: Deep learning risk model could be combined with CrisPRO's biomarker platform to improve prognostic accuracy in never-smoker adenocarcinoma.
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: Speaker noted a subset of grade‑three tumors classified as low risk by the deep learning model and sought to understand the biology.
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: CrisPRO can integrate histopathology image features with molecular signatures to refine risk scores.

unresolved_questions: What histologic features drive the deep learning model's risk predictions?

talk_metadata.talk_title: Real-world outcomes of trastuzumab deruxtecan versus standard chemotherapy in HER2‑amplified microsatellite stable metastatic colorectal cancer
rhetorical_signals: Speaker said: "Given the early curve crossing, the hazard ratio should be interpreted with caution."
Speaker said: "We observed slightly higher rates of all grade interstitial lung disease and heart failure than those reported in clinical trials."

vulnerability_identified.mechanistic_blindspot: Higher incidence of interstitial lung disease not captured in trial dosing schedules
vulnerability_identified.hidden_tox_signal: Speaker noted 12% ILD and 9% heart failure rates, higher than trial reports

trial_dilution_risk.target_biology: HER2‑amplified, microsatellite stable metastatic colorectal cancer
trial_dilution_risk.flawed_enrollment_criteria: Patients receiving standard chemotherapy had higher comorbidity burden, potentially diluting comparative efficacy
trial_dilution_risk.missing_biomarker: No prospective HER2 IHC scoring beyond IHC3+, no assessment of HER2 heterogeneity
trial_dilution_risk.statistical_concern: Potential residual confounding despite propensity matching

competitive_moat_weakness.ip_vulnerability_note: Efficacy appears limited to later lines; earlier‑line benefit remains hypothesis‑generating
competitive_moat_weakness.clinical_strategy_weakness: Use in second‑line without prospective trial may limit regulatory acceptance

cognitive_dissonance: Speaker showed similar 1‑year OS (85% vs 83%) yet concluded a long‑term survival advantage for T‑DXd, despite early curve overlap

key_data_gaps: No cancer‑specific survival or progression‑free survival
Potential misclassification of exposure and outcomes in EHR data

crispro_opportunity.description: CrisPRO could help design a prospective registry with standardized HER2 testing and toxicity capture for T‑DXd in mCRC.
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: Speaker highlighted limitations of EHR coding and lack of adjudicated adverse events.
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: CrisPRO's data‑quality framework can enforce uniform biomarker and safety data capture.

cited_competitors.name: DESTINY‑CRC01
cited_competitors.context: Referenced as prior heavily pre‑treated trial showing 45% ORR

unresolved_questions: Will earlier‑line use of T‑DXd improve outcomes in a prospective trial?

talk_metadata.institution_or_pharma: University of Utah / Huntsman Cancer Institute
talk_metadata.talk_title: Cancer risk with initiation of angiotensin receptor blockers versus angiotensin‑converting enzyme inhibitors
rhetorical_signals: Speaker said: "We observed a 20% to 25% lower risk" for cancer among ARB initiators
Speaker said: "Residual confounding remains a concern"

trial_dilution_risk.trial_name_or_nct: Target trial emulation in VHA data
trial_dilution_risk.target_biology: Effect of ARB vs ACE inhibition on incident cancer
trial_dilution_risk.flawed_enrollment_criteria: Potential indication bias; patients may differ in underlying cardiovascular risk
trial_dilution_risk.missing_biomarker: No tumor molecular profiling to stratify risk

key_data_gaps: Lack of cancer stage or histology detail
No mechanistic validation of ARB effect

crispro_opportunity.description: CrisPRO could develop a molecular signature to identify patients who might benefit most from ARB therapy for cancer prevention.
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: Speaker noted consistent 20‑25% lower cancer risk across multiple cancer subtypes.
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: Integrate pharmaco‑epidemiology with genomic risk models to personalize antihypertensive choice.

unresolved_questions: What biological pathways mediate the observed cancer risk reduction with ARBs?

talk_metadata.institution_or_pharma: Taipei Medical University / Vanderbilt University Medical Center
talk_metadata.talk_title: Assisted reproductive technology and the risk of female sex hormone‑related cancers
rhetorical_signals: Speaker said: "ART exposure was associated with modest but statistically significant higher risk of breast cancer and ovarian cancer"
Speaker said: "We did not observe any major differences in diagnosis‑free mortality"

trial_dilution_risk.target_biology: Association of ART with hormone‑related cancer incidence
trial_dilution_risk.flawed_enrollment_criteria: Inclusion limited to women aged 40‑70 at 2022 cutoff, potentially missing younger ART users
trial_dilution_risk.missing_biomarker: No data on family cancer history or BMI
trial_dilution_risk.statistical_concern: Potential residual confounding from unmeasured lifestyle factors

key_data_gaps: Lack of detailed reproductive history timing relative to ART
No data on family cancer history or BMI

crispro_opportunity.description: CrisPRO could build a predictive model incorporating ART exposure, endometriosis, PCOS, and other factors to stratify breast and ovarian cancer risk.
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: Speaker demonstrated increased HR for breast and ovarian cancer after adjusting for endometriosis and PCOS.
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: Leverage multi‑omics and clinical data to refine risk scores for women undergoing ART.

unresolved_questions: Does the number of ART cycles modify cancer risk?

talk_metadata.talk_title: Developing a real‑world clinical genomic platform to deliver precision oncology in underrepresented populations
rhetorical_signals: Speaker said: "Only 14% of the world is represented in the clinical trials datasets"
Speaker said: "Our approach is modular, and you can add new AI layers and build on it rather than a static system"

competitive_moat_weakness.ip_vulnerability_note: High cost and infrastructure requirements may limit scalability in low‑resource settings
competitive_moat_weakness.clinical_strategy_weakness: Reliance on expensive WGS may impede rapid adoption in under‑served regions

key_data_gaps: Limited prospective clinical outcome data from the Indian cohort

crispro_opportunity.description: CrisPRO can provide a cost‑effective targeted sequencing alternative to WGS for under‑represented populations.
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: Speaker highlighted need for smaller, more cost‑efficient panels to capture missed populations.
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: CrisPRO's computational pipeline can maximize information from limited gene panels.

unresolved_questions: How will data governance and patient consent be harmonized across countries?

talk_metadata.talk_title: Pan‑cancer landscape of alternative lengthening of telomeres revealed by machine learning analysis of clinical sequencing data
rhetorical_signals: Speaker said: "Our model performed pretty robustly despite relying on routinely discarded reads"
Speaker said: "ALT prevalence is high in tumor types where it has been previously reported"

vulnerability_identified.mechanistic_blindspot: Model may miss ALT cases lacking sufficient off‑target telomeric reads

key_data_gaps: Lack of orthogonal validation (e.g., C‑circle assay) for many predicted ALT cases

crispro_opportunity.description: CrisPRO could integrate ALT prediction with synthetic lethal targets to identify therapeutic vulnerabilities.
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: Speaker noted ALT‑positive tumors have distinct aggressive biology and potential targetable vulnerabilities.
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: Use CrisPRO's network analysis to map ALT‑associated pathways for drug discovery.

unresolved_questions: Which novel genes identified (e.g., RB1, POT1) drive ALT in wild‑type tumors?
